title: Account tier names
excerpt: I’d like to avoid a network request if a user is not of the correct minimum tier to create custom tags. I know that the developer tier, for example, is represented as free. Given that I’m not using a paid account, I’m wo…

posts.author: riosd (Danny)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d like to avoid a network request if a user is not of the correct minimum tier to create custom tags. I know that the developer tier, for example, is represented as free. Given that I’m not using a paid account, I’m wondering what the other tiers are definitively called.

I’d imagine they’re

  • standard
  • pro
  • enterprise

but I’d like to make sure.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @riosd!

Just to be sure we are on the same page here, are you talking about the tier inside of the plan of an account?

Looking forward to hearing from you

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: riosd (Danny)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Matias.Monday: Yup, that’s exactly where.

query {
  account {
    tier
  }
}

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @riosd!

There are:

  • basic
  • standard
  • pro
  • enterprise
  • free

Let us know if you have any other questions

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
